title: HTTP Client Ownership, Context, Status, Body Limits, and Cleanup · Mastery Check
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss Go decisions for http client ownership, context, status, body limits, and cleanup and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named browser, compiler, race, network, filesystem, terminal, load, accessibility, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for reusable bounded context-aware PokéAPI client. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice…
objectives: Inject and reuse one configured http.Client or narrow transport port because transports retain connection state and support concurrent use.
Create requests with caller-owned context and derive request deadlines without hiding cancellation behind Background.
Classify success, not found, client, server, redirect, and unexpected status before domain decoding and retain bounded error evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go team handles case 7814 during a narrow-terminal ranger workflow. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove client reuse, response admission, bounded bodies, context cancellation, and cleanup; failed and canceled commands must preserve the prior admitted state; then change one toolchain, target, artifact, migration, rollback, or restore condition and reconcile the release. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Inject and reuse one configured http.Client or narrow transport port because transports retain connection state and support concurrent use.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use injected RoundTripper and httptest server cases for headers, URL, context, status, body limit, cancellation, and cleanup.
